hallucinations.description: The paper incorrectly reports the results of the augmentation magnitude ablation in the main text. It claims that 'very aggressive' augmentation degrades performance to 27.2% MAE. However, the experimental data and the paper's own appendix show that 'very aggressive' augmentation achieved 22.79% MAE, while it was the 'very mild' augmentation condition that resulted in 27.2% MAE.
hallucinations.evidence: Main text: "while very aggressive augmentation degrades to 27.2% MAE and shows lower functional similarity." Appendix: "Very mild (permutation only, 10% of neurons): 27.20% MAE... Very aggressive (scale factor 0.70–1.30): 22.79% MAE."

hallucinations.description: The paper claims that simultaneously flipping the signs of incoming and outgoing weights preserves network function for neurons with homogeneous activations, and applies this to a network with ReLU activations. However, ReLU is only positively homogeneous and is not an odd function (i.e., ReLU(-x) != -ReLU(x)). Therefore, flipping the signs of the weights around a ReLU neuron fundamentally changes the output of the network, meaning it is not a valid symmetry-preserving operation.
hallucinations.evidence: "Sign flipping: For neurons with homogeneous activations, we randomly flip signs of outgoing and incoming weights simultaneously." combined with "All 50 MLPs use the same architecture (3072–128–128–10) with ReLU activations."

hallucinations.description: The paper cites a work by Dayan et al. with a hallucinated publication year of 2026 and a fabricated ArXiv ID (2602.01083). While the authors and title correspond to a real paper published in 2024, the specific citation details provided are nonexistent.
hallucinations.evidence: "A. Dayan, Yam Eitan, and Haggai Maron. On the expressive power of permutation-equivariant weight-space networks. ArXiv, abs/2602.01083, 2026."

overall_assessment: The paper contains multiple hallucinations. It misreports its own experimental results regarding augmentation magnitude in the main text, contains a fundamental mathematical error regarding the symmetry properties of ReLU networks (which ironically causes the 'augmentation failures' observed in the results), and includes a citation with a fabricated year and ArXiv ID.
